data.title: Nvidia-backed Reflection AI raises its funding target to $2.5 billion, valuing the company at $25 billion.
data.description: Reflection, an AI startup backed by Nvidia, has raised its funding target to $2.5 billion (previously $2 billion), potentially valuing the company at $25 billion (previously $20 billion). If successful, this would be one of the largest funding rounds in the open-source AI space. Founded in 2024 by former Google DeepMind researchers Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou, and headquartered in New York, the company focuses on building a freely usable and customizable US-based open-source AI model system, aiming to create a technology network on the Nvidia chip ecosystem to rival overseas competitors. Sources indicate that JPMorgan Chase plans to participate in this round through its "Security and Resilience Program," and existing investor Disruptive is also expected to participate. (Techstartups)

data.title: Analysis: Investors are repeating the "abandoning stocks and bonds, hoarding cash" strategy seen in 2022, indicating a rise in risk aversion.
data.description: To mitigate the risks of a potential war with Iran, investors are selling off stocks and bonds and increasing their cash holdings, a strategy mirroring that employed by Russia after its 2022 invasion of Ukraine. A recent Bank of America survey of fund managers this month showed the largest jump in cash holdings in six years. Meanwhile, JPMorgan strategists said this week that the portfolio adjustments made in response to the conflict may be far from over. A report from the JPMorgan team, led by Nicholas Panigizoglou, stated that as long as geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty remains high, current below-historic cash allocations will negatively impact future stock and bond markets. Investors have been simultaneously selling stocks, bonds, and gold, favoring cash holdings. However, compared to the levels seen at the outbreak of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, cash levels in portfolios remain moderate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Investors Reassess Private Credit Amid Challenges
data.description: Investors are showing a growing readiness to withdraw funds from private credit investments as the sector encounters significant challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the shifting sentiment among investors who are reconsidering their positions in these once-niche funds. The private credit market, which has seen substantial growth in recent years, is now facing headwinds that are prompting stakeholders to reevaluate their strategies. This trend reflects broader concerns about the stability and future prospects of private credit as an investment vehicle. As the market dynamics evolve, investors are closely monitoring developments to make informed decisions about their portfolios.

data.title: Brazil Enacts Law to Integrate Seized Crypto Assets into Public Security Funds
data.description:
Brazilian President Lula has signed Law No. 15,358, which incorporates seized crypto assets into the public security funding system for expenses such as police equipment, intelligence operations, and personnel training. According to Odaily, the law permits the temporary use of these assets before final conviction, subject to court approval.

The new regulation significantly expands judicial powers, allowing authorities to freeze, block, or confiscate crypto assets during investigations. This includes restricting access to exchange accounts, digital wallets, and related platforms. Upon conviction, individuals involved will be permanently barred from using the formal financial and crypto systems.

Additionally, the law classifies the use of encrypted communication tools or privacy technologies to conceal criminal activities as an aggravating factor. It also promotes international asset recovery and intelligence sharing, while establishing a national database to integrate the financial structures of criminal organizations.

Analysts believe this move signifies Brazil's shift from viewing crypto assets as potential reserve tools to enforcement resources, strengthening efforts against organized crime, such as PCC and Comando Vermelho. It also advances the judicial system's ability to regulate and manage digital assets.

data.title: Canadian Foreign Minister Advocates for De-escalation in Iran Conflict
data.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand announced her intention to urge G-7 leaders to work towards reducing hostilities in the ongoing conflict involving the U.S. and Israel in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this call comes amid a global struggle with rising energy prices. Anand's appeal reflects growing international concern over the economic impact of the conflict.

data.title: A Reuters poll showed that most economists believe the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates 1-2 times this year.
data.description: According to a Reuters poll, 65 out of 82 economists (approximately 79%) believe the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates one or two times this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Faces Worst Month in a Year Amid Declines
data.description: The S&P 500 Index has experienced four consecutive weeks of declines, positioning it for its worst monthly performance in a year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by the index. Investors are closely monitoring economic indicators and market trends as they navigate this period of uncertainty. The persistent downturn has raised concerns about the broader implications for the financial markets.

data.title: Bittensor Emerges as Top Performer Among Altcoins
data.description: Research firm 10x Research recently highlighted Bittensor (TAO) as one of the standout altcoins over the past two weeks. According to Odaily, Bittensor has shown remarkable performance within the firm's 50+ asset chart library. As Bitcoin's market dominance slightly declines, altcoins are exhibiting early bullish signals. However, overall market momentum remains weak, with trading volumes relatively subdued. Many altcoins are gradually developing more attractive technical patterns, and their fundamentals are improving. In the current environment, successful investment relies more on selecting specific assets rather than broad allocation, making the identification of potential winners crucial.

data.title: EU Commission to Convene Gas Coordination Meetings as Needed
data.description: The European Commission has announced plans to hold meetings of the Gas Coordination Group as necessary. According to Jin10, this decision comes amid ongoing concerns about energy supply and market stability in the region. The Gas Coordination Group, which includes representatives from EU member states, industry stakeholders, and other relevant parties, plays a crucial role in monitoring and addressing issues related to gas supply and demand. The Commission's move aims to ensure a coordinated response to any potential disruptions and to maintain energy security across the European Union. This development highlights the EU's proactive approach in managing its energy resources and addressing challenges in the gas market.

data.title: OECD Secretary-General: U.S. Labor Market Remains Tight
data.description: The U.S. labor market continues to experience tight conditions, according to the Secretary-General of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD). According to Jin10, the ongoing demand for workers in various sectors is contributing to the sustained pressure on the labor market. This situation is reflective of broader economic trends that have been observed in recent months. The tight labor market conditions are influencing wage growth and employment opportunities across the country. The OECD's observations highlight the challenges faced by employers in filling positions and the potential implications for economic policy and inflation. The organization continues to monitor these developments closely as they unfold.

data.title: Ukraine's President Zelensky Thanks UK for Targeting Russian Shadow Fleet
data.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky expressed gratitude to the United Kingdom for intensifying efforts against Russia's shadow fleet. According to Jin10, Zelensky highlighted that Russia is channeling illegal oil revenues directly into its military operations, a fact that is no longer a secret. He emphasized the necessity of intercepting sanctioned tankers and seizing the oil to curb these activities.

data.title: Qatar Airways Seeks Cash Preservation Amid Fleet Grounding
data.description: Qatar Airways is exploring strategies to conserve cash as it continues to face partial grounding of its fleet. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the airline's efforts to manage financial resources during this challenging period. The grounding has impacted the airline's operations, prompting a focus on maintaining liquidity. The airline is assessing various options to ensure financial stability while navigating the ongoing situation.

data.title: Czech Arms Manufacturer CSG Eyes Expansion Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Czech arms manufacturer CSG, owned by billionaire Michal Strnad, is exploring new business opportunities arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic focus on expanding into advanced defense technologies. CSG aims to leverage the current geopolitical situation to enhance its market presence and technological capabilities.

data.title: Fannie Mae will accept cryptocurrency mortgage loans
data.description: According to market sources, Fannie Mae will accept cryptocurrency mortgages for the first time. Fannie Mae (Federal National Mortgage Association), founded in 1938, is a government-sponsored enterprise (GSE) headquartered in Washington, D.C. Its primary business is acquiring mortgages and issuing mortgage-backed securities (MBS) to improve liquidity in the housing credit market. In 2025, it ranked 50th on the Fortune Global 500 list with revenues of $152.67 billion.

data.title: Fannie Mae to Accept Crypto-Backed Mortgages
data.description: Fannie Mae is set to accept mortgages backed by cryptocurrencies, according to BWEnews. This move marks a significant shift in the mortgage industry, as it embraces digital assets as collateral. The decision aligns with the growing trend of integrating cryptocurrencies into traditional financial systems. The acceptance of crypto-backed mortgages could potentially open new opportunities for homeowners and investors, reflecting the increasing mainstream adoption of digital currencies.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. and European Government-Bond Yields Increase Amid Renewed Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Government-bond yields in the United States and Europe have risen as inflation concerns reemerge. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that investors are closely monitoring economic indicators that suggest persistent inflationary pressures. This development has led to increased speculation about potential interest rate hikes by central banks.

In the U.S., the yield on the 10-year Treasury note has climbed, reflecting investor expectations of tighter monetary policy. Similarly, European bond markets have experienced a rise in yields, with Germany's 10-year bund yield also increasing. These movements indicate a shift in market sentiment as inflation fears prompt a reassessment of future interest rate trajectories.

Analysts suggest that the bond market's reaction is driven by recent data pointing to sustained inflation, which could compel central banks to adjust their policies. The Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank are under scrutiny as market participants anticipate their next moves in response to the evolving economic landscape.

The rise in bond yields has implications for various sectors, including equities and commodities, as investors reassess risk and return profiles. As inflation concerns persist, market dynamics are expected to remain volatile, with attention focused on upcoming economic reports and central bank communications.

data.title: EU Investigates Snap and Pornography Platforms Over Child Protection Concerns
data.description: The European Union has initiated an investigation into Snap's social network and intensified its scrutiny of several pornography platforms. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the EU's increased efforts to safeguard children in the digital space. This move is part of a broader strategy by the bloc to enhance online safety measures for minors.

data.title: Data: ETH total 24-hour contract trading volume decreased by 8.21%.
data.description: According to CoinGlass data, the total trading volume of ETH futures contracts decreased by 8.21% in the past 24 hours, with a current total trading volume of $46.333 billion. Of this, Binance's trading volume was $9.474 billion, OKX's was $8.247 billion, and Gate's was $5.443 billion. Meanwhile, the total open interest of ETH futures contracts is approximately $30.262 billion. Of this, Binance's open interest is $4.614 billion, and Gate's open interest is $3.573 billion.

data.title: Bank of England Deputy Governor Downplays Wage-Price Feedback Loop Concerns
data.description: Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden has minimized concerns about a potential wage-price feedback loop in light of the Iran War. Bloomberg posted on X, Breeden emphasized that the current weak economic conditions reduce the bargaining power of both workers and firms, suggesting that the risk of such a loop is limited. Her comments come amid ongoing discussions about the economic impacts of the conflict and its influence on inflationary pressures.
